title: tissa grandis
intro: Tissa grandis, also known as the "Bengal or Indian quinine," is a plant species native to Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant's bark and roots have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Tissa grandis is valued for its health benefits, including relieving fever and reducing pain, making it a popular herbal remedy for treating various ailments. The plant's herbal preparations include decoctions and infusions, which can be consumed as teas or used topically as ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
